message:
fix(agent): propagate error messages to event stream and handle empty OpenAI content

Two targeted fixes for message handling reliability:

1. agent.rs: When an error message is constructed after a provider
   failure, it was not being pushed to the message list or emitted
   through the event stream before steering messages were drained.
   This meant error context could be lost — the agent would silently
   move on without the caller seeing what went wrong. Now the error
   message is pushed to self.messages, added to new_messages, and
   emitted via MessageStart/MessageEnd events before steering
   message processing continues.

2. providers/openai.rs: When an assistant message has no text parts
   and no image parts, the text_content was set to None, which could
   cause downstream serialization issues with providers that require
   a content field. Changed to emit an empty string instead of None,
   matching the behavior when images are present (which uses a
   placeholder string). This prevents "missing content" errors from
   OpenAI-compatible APIs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
